description_long: This object is a vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder, featuring a rectangular body with a metallic front panel and black sides and back. The front face displays two large circular reels, each with visible tape spools, and a central tape path mechanism. Below the reels are several physical control buttons and switches, including labeled options for 'Pause', 'Play', 'Rewind', and 'Forward', as well as two green VU meters for audio level monitoring. The lower section also includes a set of sliders and indicator lights, suggesting analog audio control and monitoring functions. The overall design is robust, with a combination of metal and plastic materials, and is typical of mid-20th-century audio recording equipment.
description: A vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder with dual reels, physical control buttons, and green VU meters on a metallic front panel with black sides and back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two large tape reels, control buttons, sliders, and green VU meters.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the solid black side panel and the protruding reels.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, solid black back panel with no visible features.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a solid black panel and visible reel edges.
